title: श्री अग्नि कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अग्नि कवचम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
सप्तजिह्वं त्रिपादं च द्विशीर्षं सप्तहस्तकम्।
रक्तवर्णं यज्ञपुरुषं अग्निदेवं नमाम्यहम्॥

॥ कवचम् ॥
शिरो मे पातु वैश्वानरो ललाटं जातवेदसम्।
नेत्रे रक्षतु हव्यवाहो कर्णौ रक्षतु हुताशनः॥ १॥

नासिकां पातु सप्तार्चिर्मुखं रक्षतु पावकः।
जिह्वां रक्षतु दहनश्च कण्ठं रक्षतु शुचिप्रभः॥ २॥

स्कन्धौ रक्षतु वह्निदेवो बाहू रक्षतु तेजसाम्पतिः।
करौ पातु हविर्भोक्ता हृदयं पातु अनलः॥ ३॥

उदरं पातु चित्रभानुर्नाभिं रक्षतु विभावसुः।
कटिं पातु सर्वभक्षो ऊरू पातु शिखावहः॥ ४॥

जानुनी पातु ज्वलनो जङ्घे रक्षतु कृशानुः।
पादौ पातु सहोजाश्च सर्वाङ्गं पातु तापनः॥ ५॥

प्राच्यां रक्षतु दीप्तात्मा दक्षिणे बृहदुज्ज्वलः।
प्रतीच्यां रक्षतु ज्वालामाली उत्तरे सप्तजिह्वकः॥ ६॥

ऊर्ध्वं रक्षतु ऊर्ध्वज्वालो ह्यधस्तात्पातु मन्दरः।
सर्वतः सर्वकालं च अग्निदेवः सदावतु॥ ७॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
अग्निकवचमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेत् प्रयतो नरः।
अग्निभयं न जायेत सर्वत्र विजयी भवेत्॥

सर्वरोगविनिर्मुक्तो दीर्घायुश्च सुखी भवेत्।
अग्निदेवप्रसादेन सर्वकार्यार्थसिद्धिभाक्॥

॥ इति श्री अग्नि कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Agni Kavacham ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Saptajihvam Tripadam Cha Dvishirsham Saptahastakam.
Raktavarnam Yajnapurusham Agnidevam Namamyaham.

|| Kavacham ||
Shiro Me Patu Vaishvanaro Lalatam Jatavedasam.
Netre Rakshatu Havyavaho Karnau Rakshatu Hutashanah. || 1 ||

Nasikam Patu Saptarchirmukham Rakshatu Pavakah.
Jihvam Rakshatu Dahanashcha Kantham Rakshatu Shuchiprabhah. || 2 ||

Skandhau Rakshatu Vahnidevo Bahu Rakshatu Tejasampatih.
Karau Patu Havirbhokta Hridayam Patu Analah. || 3 ||

Udaram Patu Chitrabhanuranabhim Rakshatu Vibhavasuh.
Katim Patu Sarvabhaksho Uru Patu Shikhavahah. || 4 ||

Januni Patu Jvalano Janghe Rakshatu Krishanuh.
Padau Patu Sahojashcha Sarvangam Patu Tapanah. || 5 ||

Prachyam Rakshatu Diptatma Dakshine Brihadujjvalah.
Pratichyam Rakshatu Jvalamali Uttare Saptajihvakah. || 6 ||

Urdhvam Rakshatu Urdhvajvalo Hyadhastatpatu Mandarah.
Sarvatah Sarvakalam Cha Agnidevah Sadavatu. || 7 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Agnikavachamidam Punyam Yah Pathet Prayato Narah.
Agnibhayam Na Jayeta Sarvatra Vijayi Bhavet.

Sarvarogavinirmukto Dirghayushcha Sukhi Bhavet.
Agnidevaprasadena Sarvakaryarthasiddhibhak.

|| Iti Shri Agni Kavacham Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: अग्नि कवचम् में अग्निदेव के विभिन्न नामों से शरीर के हर अंग की रक्षा का वर्णन है। शिर की रक्षा वैश्वानर, ललाट की जातवेदस, नेत्रों की हव्यवाह, मुख की पावक, हृदय की अनल, उदर की चित्रभानु, नाभि की विभावसु करते हैं। दिशाओं में दीप्तात्मा, बृहदुज्ज्वल, ज्वालामाली और सप्तजिह्व अग्निदेव रक्षा करते हैं। पाठ करने वाले को अग्निभय नहीं होता, सर्वरोगमुक्ति, दीर्घायु और सर्वकार्यसिद्धि मिलती है।
benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish divine armor of agni dev, the fire god
Traditionally invoked for protection from fire accidents and fire-related fears
Traditionally recited for removal of diseases and grants long life
Invokes the blessing of victory and success in all endeavors
Purifies the devotee through Agni's sacred energy
description: Agni Kavacham — श्री अग्नि कवचम् — is a divine armor prayer for protection offered to Lord Agni. Rooted in Agni Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow victory and success in all endeavors and protect from fire accidents and fire-related fears. It is also said to create divine armor of Agni Dev, the fire god. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) for enhanced blessings. With 7 verses and a reading time of about 7 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Agni Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Agni Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Agni Kavacham (श्री अग्नि कवचम्) is a seven-verse Sanskrit kavacha (protective armor) prayer to Lord Agni — the Vedic god of fire, the divine messenger between humans and gods (deva-doota), and the witness (sakshi) of all Vedic yajnas. Preserved in the Agni Purana stotra tradition, the kavacham opens with a Dhyana verse meditating on Agni's iconographic seven-tongued (sapta-jihva), three-footed (tri-pada), two-headed (dvi-shirsha), seven-handed (sapta-hastaka), red-bodied (rakta-varna) yajna-purusha form. The kavacha proper then assigns each of Agni's many sacred Vedic names — Vaishvanara, Jatavedas, Havyavaha, Hutashana, Saptarchi, Pavaka, Dahana, Shuchi-prabha, Vahni, Anala, Chitrabhanu, Vibhavasu, Sarvabhaksha, Shikhavaha, Jvalana, Krishanu, Sahoja, Tapana, Diptatma, Brihadujjvala, Jvalamali, Saptajihvaka, Urdhvajvala, Mandara — to protect specific limbs and directions of the devotee's body. (Sources: Agni Purana stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is a kavacham and how does it differ from a stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: A kavacham (literally armor) is a specific genre of Sanskrit devotional composition that systematically invokes a deity's many names to protect each limb (anga-rakshana), direction (dik-rakshana), and time of day of the devotee — creating a complete protective shield (vaishnavi-armor) around the body. Each verse follows the formula X-am pātu Y or X-am rakshatu Y — let X (deity-name) protect Y (limb/direction). A regular stotra is a hymn of praise without this systematic body-mapping. Famous kavachas include the Devi Kavacham (from the Devi Mahatmya), Narayana Kavacham (Bhagavata 6.8), Hanuman Kavacham, Shiva Kavacham, Ganesha Kavacham, Surya Kavacham, Ram Raksha Stotra (which is essentially a kavacha), and this Agni Kavacham. Kavachas are recited especially before journeys, hazardous undertakings, illness, court cases, examinations, military service, and any situation requiring divine protection. (Sources: classical Sanskrit kavacha-shastra; Devi Mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: What body protections does the Agni Kavacham provide?
seoFaqs.answer: The Agni Kavacham systematically protects each part of the body through a specific Agni-name: head (shira) by Vaishvanara, forehead (lalata) by Jatavedas, eyes by Havyavaha, ears by Hutashana, nose by Saptarchi, face (mukha) by Pavaka, tongue by Dahana, throat by Shuchiprabha, shoulders by Vahnideva, arms by Tejasampati, hands by Havirbhokta, heart (hridaya) by Anala, belly (udara) by Chitrabhanu, navel (nabhi) by Vibhavasu, waist (kati) by Sarvabhaksha, thighs (uru) by Shikhavaha, knees by Jvalana, calves by Krishanu, feet by Sahoja, and the whole body (sarvanga) by Tapana. The directions are similarly protected: east by Diptatma, south by Brihadujjvala, west by Jvalamali, north by Saptajihvaka, above by Urdhvajvala, below by Mandara — and Agnideva himself protects from all directions at all times (sarvatah sarvakalam cha agnidevah sadavatu). This complete body-and-direction shield is the kavacha's protective grid. (Source: Agni Kavacham text; Agni Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Agni Kavacham be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Agni Kavacham is most powerful on Tuesday (Mangalvar — sacred to fire-warrior energy) and Sunday (Ravivar — sacred to Surya, of which Agni is the earthly form). It is also auspicious during fire-related rituals: any homa or yajna (Ganesha homa, Navagraha homa, Sudarshana homa, Chandi homa, Maha Mrityunjaya homa), Agnihotra performance, Akshaya Tritiya, Holika Dahan, Karthikai Deepam (Tamil festival of lights), and at the time of installing a new agni-kunda. Devotees recite it especially before: journeys (especially by air, train, or hazardous routes), entering high-fire-risk environments (kitchens, factories, foundries), military or firefighting service, court cases (Agni is the witness of dharma), and during illness (Agni governs the digestive fire jatharagni). The recitation should be done after morning bath, facing the sun or a lit ghee deepam. (Sources: Agni Purana; traditional kavacha recitation paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Agni have so many names like Vaishvanara, Jatavedas, Havyavaha, Pavaka?
seoFaqs.answer: Agni is the most heavily-named deity in the Vedas — the Rigveda alone contains over 200 hymns to Agni, second only to Indra. Each name reflects a specific function or domain: (1) Vaishvanara — the universal one belonging to all men (the digestive fire in every body); (2) Jatavedas — the knower of all that is born (the witness of all karmas); (3) Havyavaha — carrier of oblations to the gods (yajna-fire); (4) Hutashana — devourer of offerings; (5) Pavaka — the purifier; (6) Saptarchi / Saptajihva — seven-tongued (the seven flames Kali, Karali, Manojava, Sulohita, Sudhumravarna, Sphulingini, Vishvaruchi from Mundaka Upanishad); (7) Anala — the never-satisfied; (8) Chitrabhanu — of brilliant rays; (9) Vibhavasu — wealth-of-radiance; (10) Krishanu — the preserver; (11) Tapana — the heater. Each name protects a specific limb in the kavacha because each function of fire protects against a corresponding human vulnerability. (Sources: Rigveda Samhita; Mundaka Upanishad 1.2.4; Agni Sukta hymns.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Agni Kavacham grant the devotee?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares: Agnikavacham-idam punyam yah pathet prayato narah, agnibhayam na jayeta sarvatra vijayi bhavet, sarvarogavinirmukto dirghayushcha sukhi bhavet, agnidevaprasadena sarvakaryarthasiddhibhak — the disciplined person who recites this sacred Agni Kavacham obtains: (1) agni-bhaya-nasha — complete freedom from fear of fire (no fire accidents, fire-related calamities, or fire-induced losses), (2) sarvatra vijayi — victory in all undertakings, (3) sarva-roga-vinirmukti — freedom from all diseases (since Agni governs jatharagni, the digestive fire that determines health in Ayurveda), (4) dirgha-ayushya — long life and well-being, (5) agni-deva-prasada — the direct grace of Agnideva, (6) sarva-karyartha-siddhi — accomplishment of all worldly and spiritual goals, (7) protection through Agni's witness-power (yajna-purusha sakshi), (8) success in fire-related professions and yajna performances, (9) tejas — radiant personal magnetism, (10) sin-purification through Agni's pavaka (purifying) function. (Sources: Agni Kavacham phalashruti; Agni Purana.)
